title: Topical agents or dressings for pain in venous leg ulcers.

records.api_conc_raw: low-dose ibuprofen released from foam dressing

evidence.snippet: Persistent venous leg ulcer pain 2.1 Topical ibuprofen (two RCTs, 810 participants) Two RCTs that described the use of ibuprofen as a topical agent in the form of a foam dressing that releases low-dose ibuprofen (Biatain-Ibu (Coloplast A/S)) were included (Arapoglou 2011; Gottrup 2008).
Topical ibuprofen (two RCTs, 810 participants) ... foam dressing that releases low-dose ibuprofen (Biatain-Ibu (Coloplast A/S))

records.notes: Review-level summary only; no diffusion-cell IVPT/IVRT quantitative release data, area, or explicit endpoint values reported in selected pages.

extraction_notes: Selected pages describe clinical RCTs of ibuprofen foam dressing for venous leg ulcer pain, not diffusion-cell IVPT/IVRT experimental records. No explicit diffusion-cell parameters or release metrics were available in the provided text.

records.api_conc_raw: loaded drug; no concentration reported in selected blocks

evidence.snippet: approximately 2 ug/cm 2 after 6 hr, shown in Fig. 5 . The amount of ibuprofen released is consistent
ibuprofen’s intrinsic hydrophobic nature establishes predictable strong
provided evidence that MIL-100 and MIL-101(Fe) were suitable for drug delivery
Real time frequency data was collected using a QCM200 system (SRS)
attached with a QCM25 crystal oscillator for 1” diameter 5 MHz AT-cut

records.notes: Selected blocks indicate a QCM-based thin-film release experiment with ibuprofen release reported as ~2 ug/cm2 after 6 hr. The text route does not explicitly provide diffusion-cell or Franz-cell details, so cell_type/barrier_name are inferred cautiously from the QCM setup and marked uncertain where appropriate.

extraction_notes: Only one explicit ibuprofen release record was supported by the selected blocks. The source appears to describe a QCM thin-film release system rather than a classic diffusion-cell IVPT/IVRT, so some fields remain uncertain.

title: Design and development of a novel fused filament fabrication (FFF) 3D printed diffusion cell with UV imaging capabilities to characterise permeation in pharmaceutical formulations.

evidence.snippet: A model ibuprofen (IBU) gel formulation was tested by introducing the dosage form through the 3D printed donor compartment and the drug concentration permeated through the skin mimic (silicone membrane) was determined from the 3D printed receptor compartment using UV imaging in real-time.
A 3D printed diffusion cell in similitude of a Franz cell was successfully printed...
A 5 % w/w ibuprofen gel formulation was used as the model topical formulation.
SilatosTM silicone membrane of thickness 0.13 mm was purchased...
the silicone membrane of thickness 0.13 mm was placed on a divider between the donor and receptor compartments providing a diffusion area of 3.14 cm2.
The average permeation profile achieved for triplicate run over a 12 h period is shown in Figure 11a.

records.notes: Q_final left as null because the paper reports a time-course permeation profile and discusses limits of quantification/over-saturation, but no single explicit final concentration value at 12 h was stated in text.

extraction_notes: Single explicit ibuprofen IVPT-style record found. The paper describes a Franz-cell-like 3D printed diffusion cell with a silicone membrane; no separate multiple formulations were reported in the selected pages.

title: Controlling Supramolecular Structures of Drugs by Light.

records.dosage_form: 8 mM aqueous solution of P+-Ibuprofen ion-pair

evidence.snippet: We now studied the impact of the UV light induced changes of the P+-azobenzene Ibuprofen ion-pair on permeability. For that, the ion-pair was applied to synthetic model membranes of skin (Strat-M®-membranes).
synthetic model membranes of skin (Strat-M®-membranes).

The trans-isomer had a lag time of about 18 hours and reached equilibrium after 72 hours, whereas the cis-isomer readily penetrated through the model membrane and reached equilibrium in less than 12 hours following exponential patterns (Figure 4C).
the ion-pair was applied to synthetic model membranes of skin

records.notes: Single explicit IVPT record for ibuprofen on page 5; only one formulation clearly reported. Endpoint values are permeability time-to-equilibrium/lag time rather than flux or Q_final.

extraction_notes: No IVRT or additional clearly distinct ibuprofen formulations were reported in the selected pages. The paper discusses permeability of P+-Ibuprofen ion-pairs (trans vs cis) through Strat-M® membranes.

title: Cliniomicrobiological evaluation of the efficacy of local delivery of moxifloxacin and ibuprofen gel as an adjunct to scaling and root planing in chronic periodontitis patients.

evidence.snippet: The in-vitro dissolution results showed that there was release of drug up to 168 h, i.e., 7 days after single application of gel into the dissolution medium.
At the end of 168 h, 96.34% of moxifloxacin and 95.45% of ibuprofen were released into the medium.
In SRP + LDD group, along with SRP, LDD of 0.1 ml of moxifloxacin and ibuprofen gel was injected...
release of drug... into the dissolution medium

records.notes: Only one explicit ibuprofen IVRT record found. No membrane type, area, or formulation concentration reported in the selected text.

extraction_notes: The article is a clinical periodontitis study that includes an in-vitro dissolution/release result for the moxifloxacin/ibuprofen gel. No IVPT skin/membrane diffusion-cell study for ibuprofen was described in the selected blocks.
